executive_brief: The threat landscape has fundamentally shifted. AI-driven exploit development has collapsed traditional patch windows, culminating in Microsoft’s record-breaking Patch Tuesday and a coordinated wave of actively exploited zero-days across enterprise infrastructure. Simultaneously, the software supply chain is under systematic assault, with attackers compromising npm packages, IDEs, and AI coding assistants to weaponize developer trust and bypass traditional security controls. Nation-state actors are escalating critical infrastructure sabotage while cybercriminal syndicates face unprecedented legal retribution. Security teams must abandon reactive patching and implement continuous exposure validation, strict dependency isolation, and zero-trust network segmentation. The era of perimeter-based defense is over; resilience now depends on validating trust boundaries at every execution layer.

threat_stories.headline: The Developer Trust Collapse: AI Agents and Supply Chain Poisoning
threat_stories.narrative:
Attackers have successfully weaponized the modern development stack, turning trusted AI coding assistants and package repositories into primary infection vectors. The compromise of the AsyncAPI project demonstrates a sophisticated abuse of GitHub Actions, where malicious pull requests leveraged the `pull_request_target` event to exfiltrate repository secrets and publish trojanized npm packages to over two million weekly downloads [219], [254]. Within days, attackers pivoted to Jscrambler, injecting Rust-based credential harvesters directly into compiled binaries to bypass traditional static analysis [114], [254]. These supply chain strikes are no longer isolated incidents; they form a coordinated campaign targeting the foundational tools software teams rely on daily.

The attack surface expands beyond package managers into the IDEs and AI agents developers use to write code. A critical flaw in the Cursor IDE automatically executes a malicious `git.exe` binary placed in repository roots without user interaction, granting attackers immediate access to SSH keys and cloud tokens [229], [250]. Simultaneously, Anthropic’s Claude for Chrome extension suffers from persistent privilege isolation failures, allowing rogue browser extensions to silently trigger AI actions that read Gmail, clone private repositories, and manipulate connected enterprise services [171], [242], [324]. Researchers have also demonstrated that open-weight AI models can be poisoned with under $100 in compute costs, embedding persistent backdoors that trigger remote code execution through automated tool calls [317].

This convergence of supply chain compromise, IDE exploitation, and AI agent manipulation proves that traditional perimeter defenses are irrelevant to modern development environments. Attackers no longer need to breach firewalls when they can compromise the tools that build the infrastructure. The non-deterministic nature of AI agents, combined with overly permissive CI/CD workflows and weak extension sandboxing, creates a cascading failure model where a single compromised dependency or malicious prompt cascades into full environment takeover [1], [26], [80].

timeline.event: Jscrambler npm packages compromised with Rust-based infostealer
timeline.significance: Demonstrates direct binary injection to evade static analysis in widely used security tooling

timeline.event: AsyncAPI GitHub Actions workflow abused via pull_request_target to exfiltrate secrets
timeline.significance: Exposes systemic CI/CD trust model failures enabling mass supply chain poisoning

timeline.event: Cursor IDE auto-execution flaw disclosed; Claude for Chrome privilege bypass detailed
timeline.significance: Confirms AI/IDE environments execute untrusted code without validation, bypassing user consent

timeline.event: Research proves open-weight AI models can be poisoned for under $100
timeline.significance: Establishes that AI model weights are an unpatchable attack surface requiring architectural isolation

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. All organizations utilizing npm dependencies, AI coding assistants, or browser-based AI integrations face immediate compromise risk. Developer workstations, CI/CD pipelines, and connected enterprise SaaS environments are exposed to credential theft, secret exfiltration, and unauthorized code execution.
threat_stories.action_required: Immediately rotate all CI/CD secrets, npm tokens, and cloud credentials. Rebuild developer machines from verified clean images. Disable `pull_request_target` workflows or restrict secret access in PR contexts. Enforce strict AI agent sandboxing with outbound network restrictions. Audit and remove unnecessary browser extensions; disable AI extension auto-action features until vendor patches validate cross-origin isolation.

threat_stories.headline: Patch Tuesday's Record-Breaking Assault on Microsoft Infrastructure
threat_stories.narrative:
Microsoft’s July 2026 Patch Tuesday shattered all historical records, addressing 622 vulnerabilities in a single release cycle [172], [187], [218]. The unprecedented volume reflects the accelerating pace of AI-assisted vulnerability discovery, where automated systems identify, validate, and weaponize flaws before traditional CVE scoring or public disclosure cycles complete [258]. Among the patched flaws, three zero-days dominated the threat landscape, with two actively exploited in the wild against enterprise authentication and collaboration infrastructure [173], [191], [192].

The most dangerous exploits target Microsoft SharePoint Server and Active Directory Federation Services (AD FS). Attackers are actively chaining deserialization and elevation-of-privilege flaws to achieve remote code execution and domain-level compromise without user interaction [245], [284], [336]. CISA immediately added these vulnerabilities to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, mandating federal remediation within days [226], [284]. Compounding the crisis, researcher Nightmare Eclipse released LegacyHive, a local privilege escalation exploit targeting the Windows User Profile Service that bypasses standard access controls and affects fully patched systems [211], [230], [240], [263]. The exploit demonstrates that even after emergency patching, residual attack primitives remain accessible to threat actors who achieve initial footholds.

The scale and velocity of this attack wave expose a critical failure in traditional vulnerability management. Organizations cannot patch 622 flaws simultaneously while maintaining operational continuity, especially when hardware compatibility issues temporarily halt update distribution for Dell devices [200], [253]. The window between exploit discovery and widespread deployment has compressed to hours, not weeks. Security teams must shift from reactive patching to continuous risk validation, deploying virtual patching and strict network segmentation to contain exploitation during remediation windows.

timeline.event: Microsoft releases Patch Tuesday addressing 622 CVEs, including actively exploited SharePoint and AD FS zero-days
timeline.significance: Confirms AI-driven exploit acceleration has overwhelmed traditional patch cadences

timeline.event: CISA adds SharePoint and AD FS flaws to KEV catalog; Microsoft halts updates for affected Dell hardware
timeline.significance: Federal mandate forces immediate remediation; hardware incompatibility leaves systems exposed to known exploits

timeline.event: Nightmare Eclipse publishes LegacyHive PoC targeting Windows User Profile Service
timeline.significance: Demonstrates persistent privilege escalation paths that survive standard patching cycles

timeline.event: CISA adds CVE-2026-58644 (SharePoint deserialization RCE) to KEV with strict compliance deadline
timeline.significance: Validates active exploitation chains targeting enterprise collaboration infrastructure

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. All on-premises SharePoint, AD FS, and Windows environments face immediate compromise risk. Federal agencies face strict compliance deadlines. Organizations with Dell hardware face a dangerous patching gap during active exploitation campaigns.
threat_stories.action_required: Deploy July 2026 cumulative updates immediately across all supported endpoints. Isolate internet-facing SharePoint instances and enforce strict conditional access. Enable AMSI integration for SharePoint web applications. Monitor ProfSvc activity and restrict local administrative privileges to mitigate LegacyHive exploitation. Implement network-level virtual patching for systems unable to update immediately.

threat_stories.headline: Emergency Shutdowns: Zero-Days Cripple File Sharing and Remote Access Infrastructure
threat_stories.narrative:
Enterprise infrastructure security suffered a severe setback as two major vendors issued emergency directives to completely shut down internet-facing components due to actively exploited zero-day vulnerabilities. Progress Software ordered customers to manually power off ShareFile Storage Zone Controllers after confirming a high-severity flaw was being weaponized in the wild [53], [57], [63], [96]. The vulnerability mirrors historical unauthenticated remote code execution patterns, allowing attackers to bypass authentication and gain full control over on-premises file storage systems integrated with cloud authentication [63], [159]. The directive to pull the plug underscores the absence of viable mitigations and the immediate risk of data exfiltration and lateral movement.

Simultaneously, SonicWall warned of coordinated exploitation against its SMA1000 Secure Mobile Access appliances, targeting two distinct zero-day flaws [188], [199], [205]. CVE-2026-15409 enables unauthenticated server-side request forgery that escalates to arbitrary command execution, while CVE-2026-15410 allows post-authentication code injection within the management console [206], [226]. Attackers chain these vulnerabilities to bypass perimeter defenses, steal internal configurations, and establish persistent backdoors into corporate networks. CISA immediately added both flaws to the KEV catalog, recognizing their role in active intrusion campaigns [226].

These incidents reveal a critical vulnerability in enterprise remote access and file synchronization architectures. Internet-facing controllers and appliances remain primary targets because they bridge external threat actors with internal networks. When zero-days strike these components, organizations face an impossible choice: maintain connectivity and risk compromise, or enforce emergency shutdowns and halt business operations. The convergence of these attacks demands a fundamental redesign of remote access trust models, prioritizing zero-trust micro-segmentation and ephemeral access over always-on perimeter appliances.

timeline.event: Progress warns ShareFile admins of credible external threat targeting Storage Zone Controllers
timeline.significance: Indicates active reconnaissance and imminent exploitation of enterprise file sharing infrastructure

timeline.event: Progress issues emergency directive to manually shut down internet-facing Storage Zone Controllers
timeline.significance: Confirms zero-day exploitation with no available configuration workarounds

timeline.event: Progress confirms zero-day flaw behind the disruption; SonicWall warns of SMA1000 exploitation
timeline.significance: Validates coordinated targeting of remote access and file synchronization endpoints

timeline.event: CISA adds SonicWall SMA1000 zero-days to KEV catalog; patches released
timeline.significance: Federal recognition of active exploitation mandates immediate remediation for government and enterprise sectors

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Organizations relying on Progress ShareFile Storage Zone Controllers or SonicWall SMA1000 appliances face immediate service disruption or network compromise. Internet-facing remote access infrastructure is actively targeted by threat actors with functional exploit tooling.
threat_stories.action_required: Comply with vendor directives to isolate or shut down affected controllers until patched. Apply SonicWall platform-hotfix versions 12.4.3-03453 or 12.5.0-02835 immediately. Restrict management interface access to trusted IP ranges. Implement network segmentation to prevent lateral movement from compromised remote access appliances. Validate all file sharing integrations for unauthorized credential extraction.

threat_stories.headline: The Crypto-Targeted Malware Surge: Frameworks, Flaws, and Social Engineering
threat_stories.narrative:
Cybercriminal operations have evolved from crude ransomware drops to sophisticated, modular malware frameworks specifically engineered to target cryptocurrency holdings and enterprise credentials. The OkoBot framework represents this shift, deploying a multi-stage infection chain that establishes SSH tunnels for command-and-control, bypasses UAC, and injects malicious browser extensions to harvest seed phrases from Trezor and Ledger hardware wallets [194], [252], [291]. The framework utilizes specialized implants like SeedHunter and OkoSpyware to capture keystrokes, screen recordings, and clipboard data, exfiltrating everything via encrypted PowerShell channels. This level of operational maturity demonstrates that financial threat actors now possess enterprise-grade malware development capabilities.

Parallel to framework evolution, attackers are exploiting fundamental cryptographic failures and social engineering techniques to drain digital assets. The Ill Bloom vulnerability, stemming from weak random number generation in cryptocurrency wallet software, allows attackers to predict recovery seed phrases and systematically drain wallets, resulting in millions in unauthorized transfers [8], [21]. Simultaneously, the ClickLock and ACR Stealer campaigns weaponize ClickFix social engineering, tricking macOS users into pasting malicious shell scripts that terminate applications until login credentials are surrendered [313], [319], [351]. These campaigns bypass traditional malware signatures by relying entirely on user trust and terminal execution, proving that human manipulation remains the most reliable initial access vector.

The convergence of cryptographic weaknesses, modular malware frameworks, and refined social engineering creates a multi-layered threat environment. Attackers no longer rely on a single exploit; they combine weak RNG prediction, browser extension injection, and terminal-based social engineering to ensure comprehensive data theft. Organizations and individual users must treat cryptocurrency infrastructure with the same rigor as enterprise financial systems, enforcing hardware-enforced security boundaries and eliminating terminal execution privileges for standard users.

timeline.event: Ill Bloom vulnerability actively exploited to drain cryptocurrency wallets; GigaWiper multi-wiper analyzed
timeline.significance: Confirms systemic cryptographic failures in wallet software and escalation to destructive system sabotage

timeline.event: OkoBot framework detailed, showcasing browser extension injection and seed phrase phishing
timeline.significance: Demonstrates modular, enterprise-grade malware targeting cryptocurrency infrastructure

timeline.event: ClickLock and ACR Stealer campaigns scale, leveraging ClickFix social engineering on macOS
timeline.significance: Validates that terminal-based social engineering bypasses traditional endpoint protection

timeline.event: Continued expansion of ClickFix lures targeting Microsoft 365 and enterprise credentials
timeline.significance: Confirms social engineering campaigns are scaling beyond crypto into enterprise identity theft

threat_stories.impact_assessment: High to Critical. Cryptocurrency holders, macOS users, and enterprises with remote workforce policies face immediate credential theft and financial loss. Modular malware frameworks enable persistent access and automated data exfiltration.
threat_stories.action_required: Migrate high-value assets to hardware wallets with verified secure elements and enforce PIN/biometric protections. Restrict PowerShell execution policies to Restricted or AllSigned. Implement application allowlisting to block unauthorized terminal script execution. Monitor for suspicious browser extension installations and SSH tunnel configurations. Block outbound traffic to known Telegram C2 infrastructure and malicious domains associated with ClickFix campaigns.

threat_stories.headline: State-Sponsored Sabotage and the Era of Cyber Justice
threat_stories.narrative:
Geopolitical cyber operations have escalated from covert espionage to overt infrastructure sabotage, with Russian state actors directly targeting critical energy and transportation networks. The EU and UK imposed joint sanctions on Russian FSB and GRU units responsible for cyberattacks on Poland’s power grid, marking a significant shift toward coordinated international attribution and economic retaliation [82], [83], [98], [103]. Threat actors are exploiting legacy network device vulnerabilities, including default SNMP community strings and enabled Cisco Smart Install features, to extract configurations and establish persistent access to industrial control environments [103], [137], [237]. These campaigns prioritize operational disruption over data theft, leveraging misconfigured infrastructure to pivot into OT networks and threaten physical safety.

Simultaneously, the legal landscape for cybercrime is undergoing rapid transformation. Two members of the Scattered Spider group were sentenced to 5.5 years in prison for their role in the £29 million Transport for London cyberattack, demonstrating that law enforcement can successfully trace and prosecute financially motivated infrastructure extortion [292], [293], [307], [314], [320]. Additional convictions include a former ransomware negotiator sentenced to 70 months for aiding BlackCat attacks, and US authorities charging operators of Russian bulletproof hosting services [9], [11], [18], [177], [202]. These prosecutions signal a coordinated global effort to dismantle the enablers and executives of cybercriminal ecosystems, moving beyond takedowns to direct accountability.

The dual trajectory of escalating state sabotage and increasing legal retribution forces organizations to adapt their threat models. Nation-state actors will continue targeting legacy network infrastructure and OT environments, while financially motivated groups face higher operational risks from law enforcement. Security teams must prioritize network device hardening, disable legacy protocols, and implement strict segmentation between IT and OT environments. The convergence of geopolitical cyber warfare and domestic cyber justice creates a volatile landscape where infrastructure resilience directly impacts national security and corporate continuity.

timeline.event: EU and UK impose joint cyber sanctions on Russian FSB/GRU units over Poland grid attack
timeline.significance: Establishes coordinated international attribution and economic retaliation for infrastructure sabotage

timeline.event: US and allied governments warn of Russian campaigns targeting critical infrastructure routers
timeline.significance: Confirms active exploitation of legacy SNMP and Cisco Smart Install flaws for OT pivoting

timeline.event: Scattered Spider members sentenced to 5.5 years for TfL hack; Qantas breach details disclosed
timeline.significance: Demonstrates successful prosecution of infrastructure extortion and social engineering breaches

timeline.event: New Russian campaign deploys trojanized Zoom/WebEx installers to push Starland RAT
timeline.significance: Confirms ongoing state-sponsored supply chain deception targeting enterprise communications

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Critical. Energy, transportation, telecommunications, and defense sectors face immediate infrastructure sabotage risk. Legacy network devices and OT environments are primary targets. Financially motivated groups face increased legal exposure but continue targeting enterprise communications.
threat_stories.action_required: Disable SNMPv1/v2 and migrate to SNMPv3 with authPriv. Disable Cisco Smart Install on all network devices. Implement strict network segmentation isolating OT from IT environments. Verify digital signatures and file hashes for all downloaded enterprise software. Deploy EDR with memory scanning to detect trojanized installers and in-memory RATs. Monitor for anomalous configuration extraction and lateral movement patterns.

actor_profiles.name: Scattered Spider
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Executed sophisticated social engineering and infrastructure extortion campaigns, culminating in the £29 million Transport for London hack. Two members were sentenced to 5.5 years in prison, marking a major law enforcement victory against financially motivated infrastructure targeting.

actor_profiles.name: ShinyHunters
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Conducted year-long campaigns abusing OAuth trust relationships and third-party integrations to compromise corporate Salesforce environments without exploiting platform vulnerabilities. Leveraged misconfigured scopes to exfiltrate data for extortion.

actor_profiles.name: UAT-11795
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Distributed trojanized NSIS installers for Zoom, WebEx, and developer tools to deploy Starland RAT and WLDR memory-only implants. Utilizes blockchain-anchored C2 fallback via Polygon Ethereum smart contracts to evade traditional network monitoring.

actor_profiles.name: Nightmare Eclipse / Chaotic Eclipse
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Published LegacyHive, a local privilege escalation exploit targeting the Windows User Profile Service. The PoC demonstrates bypass of registry access controls on fully patched systems, highlighting residual attack primitives surviving standard patching cycles.

actor_profiles.name: GigaWiper Operators
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Developed GigaWiper, a destructive backdoor merging multiple wiper and ransomware codebases into a single package. Designed for system-level sabotage and on-demand destruction, targeting Windows environments with multi-stage payload delivery.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Elevation-of-privilege vulnerability allowing remote unauthenticated exploitation. Despite moderate CVSS, the flaw enables network-based privilege escalation and is actively chained in exploitation campaigns.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply July 2026 Patch Tuesday updates immediately. Enable AMSI integration and rotate SharePoint machine keys.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Insecure deserialization vulnerability enabling remote code execution. Initially rated less likely to exploit, now confirmed actively used for post-exploitation and persistence.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply latest Microsoft security patches. Implement network segmentation and strict egress filtering.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Critical deserialization flaw allowing unauthenticated remote code execution. Added to CISA KEV catalog with strict federal remediation deadlines.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Deploy July 2026 cumulative updates immediately. Federal agencies must remediate by July 19, 2026.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Elevation-of-privilege flaw allowing attackers to gain administrator privileges. Discovered during investigation of active attacks and added to CISA KEV.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply July 2026 Patch Tuesday updates. Monitor CISA KEV compliance deadlines.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Server-side request forgery vulnerability enabling unauthenticated remote code execution via the Work Place interface.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to platform-hotfix version 12.4.3-03453 or 12.5.0-02835 immediately.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Post-authentication code injection in the Appliance Management Console allowing arbitrary OS command execution.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply SonicWall urgent security patches. Restrict AMC access to trusted administrator IPs.

critical_vulnerabilities.product: Zoom Desktop Client / VDI Client / Meeting SDK
critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improper input validation allowing unauthenticated account takeover via network access, likely exploiting mishandled deep links.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Zoom Desktop Client to v7.0.0+ and VDI Client to v7.0.10+ immediately.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improper privilege management allowing unauthenticated remote system takeover over HTTP. Actively exploited in the wild.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply latest Oracle Critical Patch Update immediately. Restrict HTTP access via WAF/firewall.

hunting_leads.title: SharePoint Deserialization & RCE Exploitation
hunting_leads.context: Attackers actively chain deserialization and EoP flaws to achieve remote code execution on SharePoint servers. Hunt for anomalous deserialization patterns, machine key theft, and unexpected process spawns.
hunting_leads.query: index=wineventlog EventCode=4688 (ProcessCommandLine="*powershell*" OR ProcessCommandLine="*cmd.exe*" OR ProcessCommandLine="*mshta.exe*") ParentProcessName="w3wp.exe" | where ProcessCommandLine != "*w3wp*" | stats count by ProcessCommandLine, ParentImage, Image
indicators: Machine key extraction via reg query

hunting_leads.title: npm Supply Chain Compromise Detection
hunting_leads.context: AsyncAPI and Jscrambler compromises demonstrate malicious package injection. Monitor for unexpected package installations, obfuscated scripts, and credential exfiltration to known C2 domains.
hunting_leads.query: source=auditd event=execve (argv0="*npm*" OR argv0="*node*") (argv1="install" OR argv1="ci") | regex argv2=".*(asyncapi|jscrambler).*" | lookup npm_threat_intel package_name as argv2 output=malicious | where malicious=="true"
indicators: Outbound connections to muckcoding.com or Telegram C2

hunting_leads.title: ClickFix & Terminal-Based macOS Stealer Activity
hunting_leads.context: ClickLock and ACR Stealer campaigns rely on users pasting malicious shell scripts into Terminal. Hunt for suspicious script execution, repeated application termination, and Telegram exfiltration.
hunting_leads.query: source=macos_security event=process_launch (process_name="sh" OR process_name="bash" OR process_name="zsh") (command_line="*curl*" OR command_line="*wget*" OR command_line="*base64*") | where user != "root" | stats count by user, command_line
indicators: Terminal execution of curl/wget with base64 decoding
Outbound HTTPS to Telegram API endpoints

hunting_leads.title: SonicWall SMA1000 SSRF Exploitation
hunting_leads.context: CVE-2026-15409 enables unauthenticated SSRF leading to RCE. Monitor management interfaces for internal metadata endpoint access and anomalous HTTP requests.
hunting_leads.query: index=proxy sourcetype=web_proxy dest=10.0.0.0/8 OR dest=169.254.169.254 (method="GET" OR method="POST") url="*/api/*" OR url="*metadata*" | stats count by src, dest, url
indicators: Internal network scanning from management IPs
HTTP requests with embedded internal IP addresses

hunting_leads.title: AI Agent & IDE Auto-Execution Abuse
hunting_leads.context: Cursor IDE flaw and AI agent prompt injections enable silent code execution. Monitor for unexpected git.exe execution from repository roots and anomalous AI tool calls.
hunting_leads.query: index=endpoint process_name="git.exe" (working_directory="*repo*" OR working_directory="*project*") (parent_process_name="Cursor.exe" OR parent_process_name="node.exe") | where process_command_line != "*--version*" | stats count by parent_process_name, working_directory
indicators: git.exe execution from untrusted repository directories
AI agent tool calls to external URLs
Unexpected SSH key access from IDE processes

{'summary': 'Malicious Go packages distributed via GitHub act as first-stage Windows loaders, executing hidden PowerShell scripts to download and decrypt secondary payloads (infostealers, RATs, cryptominers) from dead-drop infrastructure.', 'severity': 'high', 'affected_products': ['Go module ecosystem', 'GitHub repositories', 'Windows operating systems'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': '', 'workarounds': ['Use Go module proxy to filter known malicious packages', 'Implement application whitelisting to block unsigned or suspicious executables', 'Monitor and alert on synthetic GitHub Actions commit patterns', 'Restrict PowerShell execution policies and disable certutil decoding capabilities'], 'references': ['SecurityAffairs: 222 GitHub Repositories Linked to Fake Go Package Malware Operation', 'Socket Security Research Report on Operation Muck and Load', 'GitHub Security Team Advisory', 'Go Security Team Module Proxy Blocklist'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': "The campaign, tracked as Operation Muck and Load, abuses the Go module ecosystem and GitHub infrastructure to distribute malware. statistics.key_changes: Microsoft patched a record 622 vulnerabilities in July 2026, including multiple actively exploited zero-days. CISA issued emergency directives for SharePoint, AD FS, SonicWall, and Oracle flaws, compressing federal remediation windows to days. AI-driven exploit discovery has collapsed traditional 43-day median patch times, forcing a shift to continuous exposure validation.
emerging_trends: AI-assisted malware development and supply chain poisoning (npm, IDEs, browser extensions)
Zero-trust bypass via OAuth abuse and misconfigured third-party integrations
State-sponsored infrastructure sabotage targeting legacy network protocols (SNMP, Smart Install)
Social engineering campaigns weaponizing terminal execution and ClickFix techniques
Blockchain-anchored C2 fallback mechanisms evading traditional network monitoring
declining_threats: Basic phishing campaigns without multi-stage payload delivery
Unpatched legacy ransomware variants without double-extortion capabilities
Perimeter-based intrusion attempts without initial access via trusted integrations
